steps.title: Map the hypothesis to predicted responses
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts an inverse relationship: birds with narrower fields of vision should be more sensitive to alarm calls; birds with wider fields of vision should be less sensitive. M. barbatus forages in dense vegetation (narrow vision) and should react to the calls. P. saturatus forages in open areas (wide vision) and should not react.

steps.title: Match the observation to the prediction
steps.content: Choice D shows P. saturatus displaying no reaction and M. barbatus displaying predator-avoidance behavior. The other choices either show both species responding the same way, compare irrelevant species, or reverse the expected pattern. That matches choice D — D is correct.
